title: 10 best herbal essential oils for coughing fits
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for coughing fits are plant-based oils that help alleviate and soothe coughs, providing relief from the discomfort and distress associated with this condition. The best herbal essential oils for coughing fits include Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Thymus vulgaris, Echinacea purpurea, Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, and Hyptis suaveolens. If left untreated, persistent coughing fits can lead to sleep deprivation, fatigue, and decreased productivity, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues. The oil's expectorant qualities also help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the herbal oil's antiviral and antibacterial compounds work to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the coughing fits, providing natural relief and promoting a quicker recovery.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Coneflower essential oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughing fits.

Expectorant properties: The essential oil contains compounds like caryophyllene and beta-elemene, which have been shown to exhibit expectorant activity, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Coneflower essential oil's antiviral and antimicrobial properties may help combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause coughs, such as the common cold and bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving coughs and congestion.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that can help soothe irritated respiratory tissues and reduce coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the echinacea roots using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 8 hours.
Grind the dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steam distill 100g of the ground echinacea root mixture with 1l of distilled water for 2 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil from the condenser column within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs. The antimicrobial compounds present in thyme oil also help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. Additionally, thyme oil's calming and soothing properties can provide relief from irritation and inflammation in the throat, allowing for easier breathing and a more comfortable coughing experience.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and expectorant properties: Thyme essential oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, which can provide relief from stubborn coughs.

Antimicrobial and antiviral properties: Thyme essential oil has been shown to have potent antimicrobial and antiviral effects, which can help to combat infections that may be contributing to a persistent cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the throat and chest, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated respiratory tracts.
Leaves: Fresh thyme leaves contain cineole, a natural decongestant that helps relieve congestion and reduce the severity of coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from thymus vulgaris plants in the morning after dew has dried for maximum oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested thyme leaves by gently shaking off any dirt or debris and air drying for at least 24 hours.
Steam distill the cleaned and dried thymus leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with water at a ratio of 1 part thyme to 4 parts water for 2-3 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled thyme leaves by decanting it into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the oil in an airtight container.
Filter the collected essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment before storing it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural decongestant and expectorant properties. When inhaled, peppermint oil can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughs. The menthol content in peppermint oil also acts as a natural analgesic, providing temporary relief from coughing fits by numbing the throat and chest. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help to soothe an irritated throat, providing quick and effective relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_properties: Decongestant property: Peppermint essential oil helps to reduce mucus congestion in the airways, making it easier for the body to expel phlegm and relieve coughs.

Expectorant property: The menthol content in peppermint essential oil helps to thin and loosen excess mucus, allowing for easy clearance from the lungs and throat, which can help alleviate coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and airways, which can contribute to persistent coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to ease congestion and soothe a sore throat.
Leaves: The leaves are utilized because they contain menthone, a compound that can help to calm a cough and reduce inflammation in the chest.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves by cutting off stems just above nodes using scissors during morning dew.
Rinse harvested leaves with cold running water to remove dirt and debris then gently shake off excess moisture.
Place 500g of fresh peppermint leaves into a large glass jar and cover with 750ml of high-proof grain alcohol ensuring leaves are completely submerged.
Allow mixture to steep for 7-10 days in a dark cupboard or on a shelf protected from direct sunlight shaking jar daily.
Strain mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container discarding solids and filter resulting essential oil using paper filters if necessary.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural decongestant properties, which help to clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways. The calming and soothing effects of lavender oil also help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, providing instant relief from discomfort and congestion. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of lavender oil help to combat infection-causing bacteria, further reducing the severity and frequency of coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: English Lavender essential oil helps to relax and calm the muscles in the throat, which can provide relief from spasms and coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of English Lavender essential oil can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, which can contribute to coughing fits.
Antiviral property: English Lavender essential oil has antiviral properties that can help to combat viral infections such as the common cold or flu, which are often associated with coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain a high concentration of volatile compounds, particularly camphor and linalool, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making them effective in relieving coughing fits.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in essential oil, containing flavonoids and terpenes that provide anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to ease congestion and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator or sun drying method at 60°f for 24 hours.
Use a coffee grinder to pulverize 100g of dried lavender flowers into fine powder.
Steam distill the powdered lavender with water at a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 hours.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil using cheesecloth or paper filters, yielding approximately 20ml.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The oil's eucalyptol content helps to relieve congestion and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughs. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, providing fast relief from stubborn coughs. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils have been traditionally used to soothe coughs and provide quick relief for those suffering from colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax and calm the muscles in the throat and lungs, reducing coughing fits by soothing inflammation and spasms.

Expectorant property: The expectorant property of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the body and providing relief from congestion and coughing.
Anti-inflammatory property: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, which can contribute to chronic coughs and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe coughs.
Barks: Containing eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that relieves congestion and opens airways to ease coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves in large quantities.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or machine drying method for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a laboratory setting for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decantation and filtration methods.
Store the extracted eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with coughing fits because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. This natural ingredient also reduces inflammation and irritation caused by coughs, providing relief from persistent hacking and clearing of phlegm. As a result, licorice essential oil can help to calm and quiet even the most stubborn coughing fits, offering natural and effective relief for those plagued by chronic coughing.
remedy_properties: Soothes mucous membranes: Herbal licorice essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from persistent coughing and congestion.
Expectorant: The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of coughing fits.

Antiviral and antibacterial: Herbal licorice essential oil has antiviral and antibacterial properties that help combat infections and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with coughs, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm coughs, making it an effective ingredient in essential oils.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high concentration of licoricidin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh licorice root, clean and chop it into small pieces.
Steam distill the chopped licorice root in a double boiler for 3 hours at 100°c.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a paper coffee filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing them in a cool place.
Allow the essential oils to mature for 7-10 days before using or bottling them for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural soothing properties, which can help to calm the airways and reduce inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm also help to combat infections that can cause persistent coughing. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties can help to relieve congestion and make it easier to breathe, providing relief from coughing fits. By incorporating lemon balm essential oils into your daily routine, you may find significant improvement in reducing the frequency and intensity of your coughs.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil helps relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and alleviating coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: Lemon balm essential oil has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil possesses antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help combat underlying infections or viruses causing coughing fits, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are used to make essential oils that help soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing coughing fits.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain compounds that ease coughing spasms and inflammation in the throat, making them a popular choice for natural remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Wash the plant material thoroughly with distilled water and gently pat dry using a clean towel.
Combine 500g of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 1000ml of high-proof industrial ethanol in a large glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the mixture twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discard the solids and transfer the resulting essential oil to a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The oil's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe the throat and lungs, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with coughs. Additionally, sage's decongestant properties help to reduce congestion and relieve pressure in the chest and sinuses, providing fast and effective relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Sage essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of various viruses and bacteria, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: Sage oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from coughs and congestion caused by excess phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, which can contribute to chronic coughing and respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, sage roots are used to create a soothing oil that helps relieve cough and cold symptoms.
Buds: Containing high levels of antioxidants and volatile oils, sage buds help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and ease coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source ensuring approximately 100 grams of material.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and dry environment for a minimum of 72 hours without direct sunlight exposure.
Combine the dried salvia officinalis leaves with 500 milliliters of high-quality carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Steam-distill the mixture using a copper alembic still with a condenser for 2-3 hours monitoring temperature and pressure controls carefully.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated throats. The oil's calming effect on the respiratory system also helps to reduce congestion and relieve tightness in the chest, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, lemon verbena has a mild expectorant property that helps loosen and clear out mucus, providing quick relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Lemon verbena essential oil has been shown to reduce the frequency and severity of coughs by soothing the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for cough suppression.

Expectorant properties: The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and chest tightness that can exacerbate coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing fits caused by conditions such as bronchitis or pneumonia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon verbena plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate respiratory issues, making them an effective ingredient for soothing coughs.
Leaves: Lemon verbena leaves contain camphor, a natural decongestant that helps relieve congestion and reduce cough severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning to ensure maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 6-8 hours or air dry it for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with carrier oils such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:4.
Steam distill the plant material-carrier oil mixture in a dedicated essential oil distiller at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours to extract the oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel into dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at 5°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the irritated throat and chest tissues. The oil's decongestant properties also help to relieve congestion and reduce mucus production, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm from the lungs. Additionally, lemongrass essential oil has natural antiseptic properties that help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses, providing fast relief from coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Decongestant property: Lemongrass essential oil helps to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure, which can exacerbate coughs by relieving blockages in the airways.

Expectorant property: The expectorant properties of lemongrass essential oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and bronchial tubes, making it easier to expel phlegm and reduce coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by coughing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick and fleshy rhyzomes are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing coughs and relieving congestion.
Roots: Lemongrass roots contain bioactive compounds that help to relax the respiratory tract muscles, providing relief from coughing fits and promoting easy breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has dried off.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and mild liquid soap to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill 20 grams of cymbopogon citratus leaves with 100 grams of still-distilled water for 60 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles.
Store the final essential oil in a cool dry place and use within six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat coughing fits with herbal essential oils is to blend eucalyptus oil with peppermint oil and lavender oil. Add a few drops of this blend to a humidifier or inhale directly from a cloth or handkerchief. The menthol in eucalyptus oil helps ease congestion, while peppermint oil's expectorant properties loosen mucus, and lavender oil calms the throat and airways. This natural remedy provides fast relief from coughing fits without harsh chemicals or side effects.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to coughing fits that are treated with herbal essential oils are bronchitis, asthma, and pneumonia. Eucalyptus, peppermint, and tea tree oils are commonly used to relieve congestion and open airways, while thyme and oregano oils have antibacterial properties that help combat infections. These oils can be inhaled through steam inhalation, applied topically, or added to a diffuser to provide quick and natural relief from respiratory issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like eucalyptol, which helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion, making it easier to cough up. This helps to clear airways and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages, providing relief from coughing and promoting a smoother breathing experience.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Acts as a bronchodilator and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relieve congestion and ease coughs by opening up airways.
Cineole: Exhibits expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus and other debris from the respiratory tract, thereby reducing cough frequency.
α-Pinene: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can trigger coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oil in Eucalyptus globulus, known for its decongestant and expectorant properties.
Buds: Eucalyptus oil extracted from buds is often used to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its strong decongestant properties.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus contain a small amount of essential oil, which is often used in combination with other parts to create a blend for respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves from eucalyptus globulus trees in the early morning when the oil content is highest.
Steam distillation of 50 kilograms of fresh leaves is required for 4 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water by decantation or centrifugation within 30 minutes.
Store the extracted eucalyptus globulus oil in airtight containers to preserve its quality and fragrance.
Label and date the containers and keep them in a cool dark place to maintain the oil's potency and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways. This reduction in inflammation leads to a decrease in coughing fits, as the body is no longer irritated. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from congestion and coughing. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils a popular natural remedy for coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Licodione: Licodione helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which in turn alleviates coughing fits.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, making it effective in soothing irritated tissues and calming coughs.
Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin exhibits expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear mucus from the airways, thereby providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are primarily used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.

Roots (again due to its high glycyrrhizin content): The roots are also valued for their ability to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making them an effective treatment for coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning to ensure optimal moisture content and freshness.
Steam distill the harvested roots at 100°c for 3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it using activated charcoal to remove impurities and contaminants.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality and shelf life.
Label the bottle with the name of the plant species, date of distillation, and concentration of the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil contains thymol, a compound that breaks down mucus and reduces inflammation in the airways, making it easier to cough up. This relief helps alleviate congestion and soothe the throat, providing quick and effective relief from coughing fits. Additionally, Thymus vulgaris essential oils has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thus relieving coughing fits.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can contribute to coughing fits.
Piperitone: Demonstrates antitussive and expectorant properties, helping to suppress coughing and facilitate the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of thyme essential oil, which is commonly used to relieve coughs due to its decongestant and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain a higher concentration of essential oils than leaves, making them a popular choice for aromatherapy and natural remedies for coughs.
Stems: Thyme stems, often harvested along with the leaves, contribute to the essential oil's therapeutic properties, including its ability to soothe and calm coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the early morning or late evening when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 weeks or until brittle.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a stainless steel distillation apparatus at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and residue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms, including persistent coughing. The oil's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work together to soothe the respiratory tract and calm the cough reflex, providing relief from coughing fits and promoting a faster recovery. Its antibacterial properties also help to combat underlying infections.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Phenylethanoids: They exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, leading to relief from coughing fits.

Cichoric acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, ultimately providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate coughing.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe a cough.
Leaves: Contain echinacoside, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects that can aid in cough relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves at the peak of potency when they are in full bloom.
Steam distill the fresh plant material at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil's menthol content helps to thin and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughs. Its anti-inflammatory properties also soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, the invigorating aroma of peppermint essential oil can help to open up airways and ease congestion, providing fast and effective relief from coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: A terpene that helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from coughing fits.
Menthone: A terpene with expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
Pulegone: A monoterpene with antispasmodic properties, which helps to relax the muscles in the airways and prevent spasms that can lead to coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oil, containing high amounts of menthol and menthone, which provide anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Stems: The stems contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone than leaves, making them a valuable source for essential oil production.

Fruits: Although less commonly used, the fruits of Mentha x piperita contain essential oils with similar properties to those found in leaves and stems, providing a natural remedy for coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves of the plant in the morning after the dew has evaporated approximately 8 ounces.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with water to remove dirt and debris in about 10 minutes.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a distillation apparatus at 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus it should be colorless and have a strong minty scent.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Ginger, also known as Zingiber officinale, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have been shown to soothe the respiratory tract and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up. By reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus, ginger essential oil can provide quick relief from coughing fits, helping to clear the airways and promote a sense of calm and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: It helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing fits.
Zingiberene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease congestion and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
Germacrene D: It has expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are widely used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: The roots are also commonly used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing coughs and relieving congestion.

Buds: The buds are used for their high content of essential oils, including zingiberene and gingerol, which have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 kg of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from a well-maintained and pest-free environment.
Wash the rhizomes thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the washed zingiber officinale rhizomes at 100°c temperature for 2 hours to extract oil.
Collect the extracted oil and separate it from water through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the collected oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and achieve a clear consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which work together to calm the respiratory system and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs. This helps to ease coughing spasms and promote a sense of relaxation. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from coughing fits and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It has an anti-inflammatory effect that helps reduce the inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing fits.
Linalyl acetate: It has a sedative effect that can help calm the nervous system and reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits.
β-Pinene: It has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and reducing the severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their calming and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and congestion.
Flowers: They are used for their soothing and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from coughs and other respiratory issues.
Stems: They are used for their antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections and reduce coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 pounds of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers during the early morning to prevent oil degradation.
Dry the harvested flowers for 2-3 weeks in a well-ventilated area to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 5 pounds of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water and impurities through decantation and filtration.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with coughing fits because it has natural expectorant properties that promote the removal of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. The oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate coughing. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of the oil can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough, providing relief and promoting a speedy recovery. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help soothe and calm the respiratory system, providing relief from coughing fits.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for making essential oils from Rosmarinus officinalis because they contain the highest concentration of rosmarinic acid, a key compound for its therapeutic benefits.
Flowers: Flowers are often used to make essential oils because their fragrance and medicinal properties are highly prized for their decongestant and expectorant effects.
Buds: Buds may be used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of camphor and borneol, which contribute to the herb's cough-suppressing and decongestant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of rosmarinus officinalis leaves on a dry sunny morning after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the leaves for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil from the plant material.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol and other impurities.
Filter the essential oil through a charcoal filter to remove any remaining impurities or color.
Bottle the final product in dark glass containers and label it with the name and date.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, work to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, its warm and spicy aroma can help to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, promoting a sense of calm and comfort. This makes Cinnamomum verum essential oils a popular natural remedy for coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Helps to reduce inflammation and relax the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
Eugenol: Exhibits antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm the muscles in the airways and reduce coughing.
Linalool: Possesses anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and facilitate the expulsion of mucus, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamomum verum leaves are used to make essential oils that help alleviate coughs and respiratory issues due to their eucalyptol content.
Buds: Cinnamomum verum buds are used to make essential oils that provide relief from coughs and congestion due to their high concentration of cineole.
Barks: Cinnamomum verum barks are used to make essential oils that soothe coughs and respiratory issues due to their warm, spicy aroma and eugenol content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves and twigs in the early morning when they are at their highest oil content approximately 25% to 30% of plant weight.
Dry the harvested cinnamomum verum plant material in a warm well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content to around 10%.
Steam distill the dried cinnamomum verum plant material at a temperature of 100 degrees celsius to separate the oil from water using a distillation apparatus for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the extracted oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel and then store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Filter the extracted oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oils helps with coughing fits because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like pinene and limonene, which help to break down mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel from the lungs. This can provide quick relief from coughing fits caused by respiratory infections or allergies. Additionally, the oil's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties can also help to calm the throat and reduce irritation, further alleviating coughing and promoting a more comfortable breathing experience.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the throat and alleviate coughing fits.
α-Pinene: As a bronchodilator, α-pinene helps to relax the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
Bornyl acetate: This monoterpene has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby reducing coughing fits caused by congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for coughing fits due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their fragrance and medicinal properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Stems: Stems are also used to make essential oils for coughing fits, containing essential oils that have decongestant and expectorant properties to relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area at 35°c for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried hyptis suaveolens material for 2-3 hours using a steam distillation apparatus to extract essential oil.
Collect the essential oil using a separatory funnel after distillation is complete and transfer it to a clean glass container.
Store the obtained hyptis suaveolens essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
